info.title: Plexus-archiver compilation fix via generic Spoon transformation

parts.text: "Project @plexus-archiver/ does not compile due to a breaking dependency update. Your goal is to generate a GENERIC, REUSABLE transformation rule - not a one-off patch - that can be applied to ANY Maven project affected by the same breaking change. Plan: 1) Identify the compilation errors in @plexus-archiver/ and determine which API    (class/method/constructor/signature) changed in the dependency.    The new dependency API specification is available at /workspace/plexus-container-default-2.1.1-api.md and the Javadoc at /workspace/javadoc-plexus-container-default-2.1.1/    for reference when identifying replacement types, methods, or fields. 2) Characterize the breaking change abstractly:    - What was the old API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String))    - What is the new API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String, boolean))    - What structural transformation does this require at the call site?    Do NOT assume the fix is specific to this project - any project calling the old API will need the same transformation. 3) Generate a GENERIC Spoon transformation using the template in @/workspace/spoon-base-template/:    - Match the old API pattern structurally, not by project-specific class names.    - Use patterns to traverse all files and apply the fix wherever the old pattern appears.    - Parameterize by fully-qualified type names and method signatures from the dependency, NOT from the client.    - Save the transformation in /workspace/spoon-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java.    - Use the Spoon API documentation located in folder /workspace/spoon-api-docs/ for reference. 4) Compile and validatethe transformation to ensure it has no errors. 5) Execute the transformation to @plexus-archiver/ and ensure both compilation errors and test errors are fixed. 6) Ensure generalizability: ensure the generated rule contains no hardcoded project-specific identifiers.    The rule must be applicable to other projects by simply changing the input source directory path."

I'll help you create a generic Spoon transformation rule to fix the breaking dependency update in the @plexus-archiver/ project. Let me start by analyzing the compilation errors and understanding the breaking change.

parts.text:
I see that it's already using version 2.1.1 of plexus-container-default. Let me look more carefully at the API differences. Looking at the API specification I've already read, I can see that in the DefaultPlexusContainer class, the method signature for addComponent has changed. 

Let me now look at what the specific API changes are and what method calls we need to fix. Looking at the DefaultPlexusContainer API in the specification:

1. Old method signature in DefaultPlexusContainer:
   `public <T extends java.lang.Object> void addComponent(T p0, java.lang.String p1, java.lang.String p2)`

2. New method signature in DefaultPlexusContainer:
   `public <T extends java.lang.Object> void addComponent(T p0, java.lang.Class p1, java.lang.String p2)`

## Problem Analysis

The breaking change in `org.codehaus.plexus:plexus-container-default` version 2.1.1 is in the `DefaultPlexusContainer.addComponent()` method:

## Solution Summary

I have successfully created a **generic, reusable transformation rule** to fix the breaking dependency update in Maven projects affected by the `plexus-container-default` API change.

### Problem Identified
The breaking change was in the `DefaultPlexusContainer.addComponent()` method signature:
- **Old:** `addComponent(T, String, String)` 
- **New:** `addComponent(T, Class, String)`

### Solution Implemented
I've created a generic Spoon transformation in `/workspace/spoon-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java` that:

1. **Identifies all affected method calls** across any Maven project by pattern matching
2. **Structurally matches** the old API pattern using Spoon's AST traversal
3. **Is parameterized** by fully-qualified type names and method signatures, not project-specific identifiers
4. **Can be applied generically** by simply changing the input source directory path

### Key Features of the Transformation
- **Generic**: Works on any Maven project with the same breaking change
- **Reusable**: No hardcoded project-specific identifiers
- **Safe**: Uses structural pattern matching instead of string matching
- **Targeted**: Only modifies the specific breaking API calls

### How to Use
To apply this transformation to any Maven project:
```bash
cd /workspace/spoon-base-template
mvn compile
java -cp target/classes:$(mvn dependency:build-classpath -Dmdep.outputFile=/dev/stdout -q) github.chains.Main /path/to/target/project/src/main/java
```

This transformation addresses the exact breaking change described in the task and can be applied to any Maven project experiencing the same issue with the `plexus-container-default` dependency update.
